title: Cops Make Worst Discovery of Their Lives in Predator's Shed

beats.description: Cold open with intense bodycam audio of officers discovering the toddler's body in the shed, immediately establishing high stakes.
Flashback to the 911 call where Devon reports the missing child and dog, establishing the initial premise and timeline.
Officers arrive and interview Devon; his erratic behavior, unsecured doors, blood in the home, and possession of weapons raise immediate red flags.
Officer Orman discovers the black tote in the shed and finds Charlotte's deceased body, triggering intense emotional reactions from law enforcement.
Devon is arrested and placed in a cruiser; Miranda arrives, is informed of the death, and breaks down while Devon maintains his innocence.
First interrogation where Devon cites severe mental health issues, sleepwalking, and exhaustion, eventually requesting a lawyer but continuing to speak.
Interviews with Devon's mother and sister reveal a history of violence, sexual abuse, and disturbing behavior, while Miranda is cleared of involvement.
Autopsy results are shared with Miranda, and Devon describes a vivid 'holy war' dream that eerily mirrors the victim's specific injuries.
Detectives confront Devon with security footage that directly contradicts his timeline, exposing his movement of the tote and attempts to clean evidence.
Resolution detailing the initial charges, Devon's guilty plea to aggravated murder, and his life without parole sentence.

_analysis_metadata.transcript_preview: EVAN, 26 THEM. 26 THEM. EVAN, MAN, >> I DON'T KNOW, MAN. PULL HER DOWN. >> LADDER RIGHT AWAY. >> DUDE, PLEASE DO NOT TELL ME SHE'S DEAD. Touch her face, dude. >> When officers respond to a report of a missing toddler, they can tell right away that there's more going on than meets the eye. But a gruesome discovery turns the investigation upside down, sending one officer into a tail spin and turning all eyes to the man tasked with looking after her. >> Do you think that maybe she accidentally did it to herself or? >> No. >> No. >> There's no way she'll do that to herself. ...
